level_signals: 5+ years of technical program management experience
Drives cross-org decisions and owns critical path to major launches
Sets program strategy in partnership with DEeP leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of technical program management experience with demonstrated success in driving complex cross organizational programs.
Strong product sense and ability to understand enterprise customer needs

Demonstrated ability to communicate with precision and credibility across engineering, product, and executive audiences, translating complex technical trade-offs into strategic decisions, and driving alignment.
Demonstrated ability to lead and align cross-functional teams — driving decisions, resolving conflict, and building consensus across organizations with competing priorities
Proven track record of successful program delivery in complex technical environments
Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in product management or product operations
Experience with developer platforms, API products, or payment systems

scope.surface: Extensibility platform — Scripts, Custom Objects, and Workflows within the Developer Experience & Product Platform (DEeP) organization
primary_metrics_inferred: Successful launch of Scripts, Custom Objects, and Workflows
Customer retention and TAM expansion via extensibility
core_responsibilities: Drive cross-org decisions across DRIs in Script Platform, Custom Objects, Apps, SDK, Observability, DevEx, and Versioning
Own the critical path to major launches — proactively shaping scope, driving descope decisions, and negotiating trade-offs across teams
Influence and shape product and engineering roadmaps at the program level
Support ProServ and Partnerships by surfacing their requirements into the roadmap
Set program strategy in partnership with DEeP leadership

green_flags: Clearly described product surface and strategic importance of the role within DEeP
Newly created, high-impact role with explicit ownership of a flagship program
Preferred qualifications are explicitly positioned as bonuses, not requirements — signals openness in hiring bar
JD describes concrete primitives (Scripts, Custom Objects, Workflows) giving a clear sense of scope
red_flags: No location or remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated anywhere in the JD
No visa sponsorship or work authorization information provided
Posting date not available — unclear how fresh the role is

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, scope of responsibilities, and strategic ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and program goals; not stated in JD)
scope.team_size_estimate (inferred from '10+ cross-functional teams' mentioned in JD body)
location.type (set to 'unclear' — no location information in JD)
